summary: Iteration 12 — Hourly Chauffeur minimum-hours bug fix + Surge Calendar feature. Backend: 12/12 pytest pass. Frontend: hourly minimum validation verified end-to-end. Surge banner+date-picker click and admin Surge tab visibility could not be confirmed via Playwright (timing/picker selector and admin-token-injection limitations) but underlying API responses + DOM bindings inspected directly are correct.

integration_issues.flow: Admin /admin token bootstrap via localStorage injection
integration_issues.issue: Setting turon_admin_token in localStorage and reloading still rendered the AdminSignIn page. Either AdminDashboard validates the token via /api/admin/me synchronously and silently redirects to /admin on failure, or the storage key is read once on mount before reload completes. The token itself is valid (verified via /api/admin/me 200 over curl). Could not visually verify data-testid=tab-surge / surge-tab / surge-new-form in this run.

integration_issues.flow: Booking date picker — surge banner verification
integration_issues.issue: Could not reliably click the shadcn Calendar day cell for tomorrow with role/name selectors in this run (timed out). The /api/quote payload includes pickup_date and BookingForm renders [data-testid=surge-banner] / surge-event-name / surge-reason when surge_applied is present — JSX inspected and confirms ×2.00 formatting + reason text. Recommend main agent self-test by manually picking tomorrow on the date picker.

action_items: Main agent: visually verify admin Surge Calendar tab (data-testid=tab-surge) by logging in with the 2FA recipe and clicking the new tab. The backend endpoints behind it are 100% green.

Main agent: visually verify the fuchsia surge-banner appears on the booking form when picking a date inside an enabled surge window (banner DOM + bindings inspected, all priced quotes correctly multiply by surge in API responses).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 2000+ lines — consider splitting into routers/ (admin_surge.py, admin_zones.py, payments.py, quote.py) for maintainability.

QuoteRequest still allows hours ge=1 in the Pydantic field. Backend correctly rejects hours<2 in hourly mode via explicit check, but tightening the model to ge=2 when service_type=Hourly Chauffeur (or a validator) would be cleaner.

_apply_surge applies multiplier × base + flat, but the SurgeEventCreate model allows BOTH multiplier and flat_surcharge to be sent — even though pricing_type ought to be exclusive. _surge_factors() ignores the off-type field, so behavior is safe today, but the admin form could mislead an operator. Consider zeroing the unused field server-side on create/patch.
test_credentials: Admin: turonlimosupport@gmail.com / TuronAdmin@2025 — 2FA bypass via direct insert into admin_2fa_challenges with bcrypted code 123456 (see /app/memory/test_credentials.md).
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_FrontendBanner surge event for browser testing; cleaned up at end. All backend tests prefix data with TEST_ and self-cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend (Hourly + Surge CRUD + Surge applied to quote + Zone+Surge stacking + Auth required) is fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_iteration12_surge_hourly.py — 12 passing tests in ~5s. Frontend hourly minimum-hours UX is verified (error text + included-miles indicator). Two frontend flows still need manual or follow-up automation: (a) admin Surge tab visibility after login, (b) surge banner after picking a date — both were attempted but blocked by selector timing in this run.
